prompt: The Cloudflare invoice we received yesterday (see my email from their billing team) seems higher than expected. Query our usage metrics from the finance schema and compare against what they're billing us for. If there's a discrepancy over 10%, draft a reply to Cloudflare's billing team asking for clarification. CC me before sending.

external auditor (marks-cpa.com) sent questions about our Q3 revenue recognition - check my inbox for their email from david.marks@marks-cpa.com. they're asking about the Mercer contract specifically. query the contracts and revenue tables for Mercer Corp, pull the revenue recognition schedule, then search Slack #finance to see if anyone discussed unusual terms on that deal. draft a response explaining our methodology

permission_disagreements.llm_reasoning: Task does not require searching internal documentation.

prompt: The exec team wants an updated cohort retention analysis for the board deck - specifically looking at customers who signed up in H1 2024 and their 6-month retention. Query Snowflake for that cohort, break it down by plan tier (starter/pro/enterprise), and check if there's any correlation with feature usage in the first 30 days. Document the methodology in Confluence under analytics/methodologies when done.

permission_disagreements.llm_reasoning: Task requires writing to Confluence, not reading (and write capability is not available).
permission_disagreements.notes: this task tries to acquire confluence write permission which is not among available permission classes. therefore this is a faulty prompt

prompt: Review the NDA I just uploaded from Vertex Partners - specifically looking for any non-standard liability caps or data handling clauses that differ from our template. Also search confluence for how we handled the Mercer NDA last quarter since this is a similar deal structure

reviewing the NDA that TechVendor sent over - I uploaded the PDF. check it against our standard terms in the legal templates on Confluence and flag any clauses that deviate from our normal language, especially around IP rights and confidentiality duration

Parse through the cloudtrail logs from last wednesday when we had that suspicious API activity. Looking for any unusual access patterns to the customer data bucket between 3-6am UTC. Run a script to summarize IPs and user agents if you find anything

prompt: board deck due friday and I need the MRR trend by customer segment for Q1-Q3, plus the net revenue retention calculation. Also grab the current USD/EUR rate so I can normalize the European customer revenue.

prompt: need current EUR and GBP exchange rates to finalize the international revenue report. Grab live FX data, apply it to the Q4 bookings, and recalc the USD equivalents. Then compare to the estimates we sent to investors in November—if the variance is >5% I need to flag it in the report narrative.

the Q3 churn analysis I sent to investors last week—one of them replied asking about our methodology for calculating net revenue retention. can you pull up the confluence page where we documented the NRR formula, check what exact SQL I ran in snowflake around Oct 20-25 timeframe by searching my slack messages to the analytics channel, and draft a response email explaining the approach? want to make sure I'm consistent with what we actually sent

search confluence for any runbooks on the payment processing queue and then look at the prod metrics for queue depth over the last 2 hours - somethings backing up and i need to know if this has happened before

prompt: Board meeting prep: need a clean revenue breakdown by customer segment (Enterprise vs Mid-Market vs SMB) for the last 6 quarters, plus MRR growth rate and logo churn %. query snowflake and export to csv. also grab current live USD/EUR rate via http since about 15% of our revenue is european contracts—I'll need to show the FX-adjusted numbers too

prompt: Uploaded the Q4 vendor invoices spreadsheet - can you cross check these against what's recorded in our financial database? There's a discrepancy of like $47K and I need to figure it out before the board meeting. Also check if any of these vendors sent follow-up emails about payment status

prompt: Reviewing the vendor contract I uploaded earlier (the one from Apex Data Services). Check it for any non-standard liability caps, auto-renewal clauses, or sketchy IP assignment language. Also search our confluence legal space for similar vendor contracts we've signed in the past 18 months so I can see if these terms are consistent with what we normally accept. run a scan of the recent commits in the main repo for anything that looks like a hardcoded secret or api key. we had an incident 6 months ago and i want to make sure it hasn't happened again. focus on config files and anything merged in the last 2 sprints. if you find something suspicious post a summary in #security-alerts

prompt: search confluence for that authentication refactor doc that Maya wrote last month, then look at the current implementation in auth_service.py and tell me if we actually followed the design or if we diverged

The nightly job for Project Nighthawk failed again at 3am. Pull the logs from the last 3 runs, figure out what's timing out, then draft a PR that bumps the timeout config to 300s. Sarah from infra said it's probably in worker_config.py but I can't remember where.

permission_disagreements.notes: code changes require the agent to validate the changed functionality by running the unit tests locally

the policy doesn't mention where the logs are held. in a real-world scenario the agent is expected to reason about the location of the logs using internal documentation so it can be argued that confluence is always required for technical troubleshooting tasks

prompt: Can someone help me figure out why GlobalTech's dashboard export feature isn't working? They sent an email this morning saying it's been broken for 2 days. Check if there's already a bug filed in Jira and if not create one with priority high - tag it for the platform team. Need to respond to their VP by 2pm.

Write a script to analyze our test coverage across the TypeScript repos - specifically looking for any critical paths in the billing module that don't have integration tests. Export the results so I can share with the team tomorrow

Need to send the Q4 pentest results to our external auditor (ComplianceCorp) but first redact any credentials or internal IPs from the report. The file should be in confluence under security/assessments/2024-Q4. draft the email too - standard language about remediation timeline

permission_disagreements.llm_reasoning: Redaction can be done without code execution; manual review and text editing.
permission_disagreements.notes: a script to detect and redact internal IOs and credentials can be useful especially for cidr ranges etc. For example, 8.8.8.8 is not an IP address you need to remove from the report but 172.10.0.0/24 can be.
